revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:阿智神社 (倉敷市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Achi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Achi Jinja5.JPG |location = 12-1 Honmachi, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34.597778|N|133.773611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Three Goddesses of Munakata|qid=Q10948069|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}} |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday in May (Spring Grand Festival)
3rd Sunday and preceding Saturday in October (Autumn Grand Festival) |location = {{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Achi Shrine'''|阿智神社|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on {{ill|Tsurugata Mountain|ja|鶴形山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugata Mountain|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugata Mountain|1=Tsurugata Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11676019}} in Honmachi, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == The shrine is situated atop the small hill known as Tsurugata Mountain, located in the northern part of the {{ill|Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|ja|倉敷美観地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|1=Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11386772}} in central Kurashiki City. Historically, this area was known as "Kamejima" or "Tsurugatajima," and it is said that the ancestors of the [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no omi]] created a garden here, which became the foundation of the shrine. In ancient times, the Okayama Plain was a part of the sea known as {{ill|Kibi no Anaumi|ja|吉備の穴海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Anaumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Anaumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500658}}, and the area around Tsurugata Mountain was a critical transportation hub. Consequently, the shrine enshrines the {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, regarded as protectors of maritime transportation. The shrine precincts include an ancient garden called the Crane and Turtle Garden, featuring numerous sacred rocks such as the "Ama no Iwasaka" and others. The garden near the "Saikan" includes yin-yang sacred rocks and a suikinkutsu (water harp cave). Other structures include a noh stage, Bashō Hall, and an ema hall. The "Achi Wisteria," located north of the honden, is famous for being the largest and oldest in Japan. The wisteria has been designated as a natural monument by Okayama Prefecture and has also been adopted as the city flower of Kurashiki. The Wisteria Festival is held annually from May 3 to May 5. The area south of the shrine, including Kurashiki Honmachi and Higashimachi (now part of the Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter), developed as a temple town and later prospered as a port town. During the Edo period, the Kurashiki Magistrate's Office was established in this area, which also flourished as a samurai district with many merchant houses. The shrine grew significantly during this time as one of the major shrines of Kurashiki Village, supported by its many wealthy parishioners. Today, it remains an important religious site attracting numerous parishioners and visitors alongside the Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * Nineteen subsidiary deities in the enshrinement: ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ** Izanagi no Mikoto ** Izanami no Mikoto ** Yagokoro Omoikane no Kami ** Uwatutsunoo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsunoo no Mikoto ** Sokotsutsunoo no Mikoto ** Amaterasu Ōmikami ** Yasokashihitohime no Kami ** Kamunaobi no Kami ** Ōnaobi no Kami ** Susanoo no Mikoto ** Kushiinadahime no Mikoto ** Ōichi Hime no Kami ** Ukano Mitama no Kami ** Ōkuninushi no Mikoto ** Sukunabikona no Mikoto ** Kunado no Kami ** Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto == Divine Virtues == The shrine is believed to grant blessings in business prosperity, traffic safety, beauty and health, artistic skill, and maritime safety. == Festivals == * May 3–5: Wisteria Festival * 3rd Saturday and Sunday in May: Spring Grand Festival * 3rd Sunday and preceding Saturday in October: Autumn Grand Festival == Gallery == {{Gallery |align=center |width=160 |height=120 |File:Achi Jinja1.JPG|First torii gate (taken September 2015) |File:Achi Shrine in Kurashiki.jpg|Kinmon Torii gate (taken September 2015) |File:Achi Jinja3.JPG|Stone steps of the approach (taken September 2015) |File:Achi Jinja4.JPG|Stone steps of the approach (taken September 2015) |File:Achi Jinja7.JPG|Haiden (taken September 2015) |File:Achi Jinja6.JPG|View of Kurashiki City from the shrine grounds (taken September 2015) }} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|ja|倉敷美観地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|1=Kurashiki Bikan Historical Quarter|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11386772}} * {{ill|Suginkyo|ja|素隠居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suginkyo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suginkyo|1=Suginkyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605658}} * {{ill|Kanryū-ji Temple (Kurashiki)|ja|観龍寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanryū-ji Temple (Kurashiki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanryū-ji Temple (Kurashiki)|1=Kanryū-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630665}} == External Links == * [http://achi.fem.jp/ Official Achi Shrine Website] {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Buddhism-stub|qq=}} {{pref-stub-Okayama Prefecture|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|阿智神社 (倉敷市)|version=100672178|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Hidden category}}}} [[ja:阿智神社 (倉敷市)]] {{Commons category|Achi-jinja (Kurashiki)}} {{wikidata link|Q11657446}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Kurashiki Bikan historical quarter]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
